title: Vulkan Architecture — Devices, Queues & Command Pools
description: Covers the complete Vulkan instance and device creation pipeline, queue family selection (graphics/compute/transfer queues), command pool allocation, command buffer recording and submission, and the VkSemaphore/VkFence synchronization primitives required for basic frame rendering. Vulkan's explicit architecture requires the programmer to make every resource state transition explicit — understanding this model is prerequisite to writing any Vulkan code without validation layer errors. Students will write a minimal Vulkan renderer from scratch (without any framework), submit a compute pass followed by a graphics pass with correct semaphore synchronization, and validate zero validation layer errors.
